title: Similarities and trends in the properties of the Group 2 metals, magnesium to barium, and their compounds
summary: 9701 AS Group 2 — reactivity, hydroxides, sulfates, and thermal stability trends with PhET reactions sim.
learningObjectives: Describe reactions of Group 2 metals with water and dilute acids.
Explain increasing reactivity down the group.
State trends in solubility of hydroxides and sulfates.
Describe thermal stability of Group 2 carbonates and nitrates.

simpleExplanation.title: Group 2 trends
simpleExplanation.summary: Reactivity increases down Group 2 as ionisation becomes easier. Hydroxide solubility increases; sulfate solubility decreases — BaSO₄ is insoluble.
steps: M → M²⁺ + 2e⁻ easier down group — faster reaction with water.
BaSO₄ insoluble — barium meal X-ray contrast; sulfate test.
Carbonates decompose more readily down group (lower ΔH_latt).

sections.content: **Group 2** (10.1) links to **thermal decomposition** and **solubility rules**. Show **ionic equations** for reactions with water and dilute HCl.
